article_meta.title: After some 150 students walked out of Redlands schools early this month in support of immigrants, they were dealt an une

reasoning: The model mixed valid author-prose bias with multiple errors: it flagged two direct quotes as author bias (attribution violations), included several questionable/neutral items as bias, and misapplied some bias-type labels. It did catch a key slanting comparison in the author’s voice but missed other plausible instances.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Two clear violations: it flagged quoted statements from an unnamed man and from a student as author bias.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most flagged items were questionable or non-biased author prose, and two were quoted text; only the first item was clearly defensible.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: It caught the main slanting comparison but missed other plausible author-level bias such as the loaded term 'crackdown' for immigration enforcement.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Where items were arguably biased, labels were often off (e.g., 'conservative-majority' as Subjective Qualifying Adjectives; 'Spin' where 'Slant' fits better; 'Omission of Source Attribution' misused).
